creative_brief: Close-up of cracked egg showing vivid orange yolk against white shell, natural lighting on rustic wooden surface.
primary_text: Last Tuesday Rachel Ward cracked an egg from her pasture-raised hens. Deep orange yolk, firm white, shell that actually resisted breaking. Compare that to supermarket eggs sitting in cold storage for weeks. Her chickens roam free on regenerative pastures in NSW, not cramped in sheds. That's why the yolks look different, taste richer. Before Christmas, get eggs that are days old, not weeks. No middlemen. Collect from Brookvale hub Monday to Friday.
headline: Why these egg yolks are bright orange
description: Rachel's pasture-raised hens in NSW. Days old, not weeks. Collect Brookvale Monday-Friday.
